root.narration: You moved up to the net and your opponent hits the ball right at you before it bounces. You need to hit a volley. What do you do?

choices.text: Keep your racket out in front, use a short punch motion to block the ball, and angle it away from your opponent
Take a big backswing and try to smash the ball as hard as you can
Let the ball bounce first, then hit it like a regular shot

outcome.headline: Clean volley winner!
outcome.explanation: At the net, you do not need a big swing. A short punch with firm wrists directed the ball into the open court for a winner. Your opponent could not reach it.
outcome.whatToRemember: Volleys are about placement, not power. Keep your swing short, your wrist firm, and aim for the open side of the court.

keyTerms.definition: A shot hit before the ball bounces, usually at the net. Volleys use a short punching motion instead of a full swing

outcome.headline: Too much swing!
outcome.explanation: Taking a big backswing at the net gives you no time to react. The ball came fast, and your big swing sent the ball flying way past the baseline.
outcome.whatToRemember: At the net, keep your swing compact. There is not enough time for a full backswing. Think punch, not swing.

outcome.headline: You let the chance slip by!
outcome.explanation: Waiting for the ball to bounce when you are at the net is a missed opportunity. The ball bounced and popped up, and by then your opponent was already in position to pass you.
outcome.whatToRemember: When you are at the net, take the ball out of the air. That is the whole point of being up there -- to finish the point quickly.
